suppliers.context: The Consolidated Financial Statements, together with the Notes thereto and the report thereon dated February 13, 2026, of PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP, the Firm’s independent registered public accounting firm (PCAOB ID 238), appear on pages 162–314.
Indenture, dated as of October 21, 2010, between JPMorgan Chase & Co. and Deutsche Bank Trust Company Americas, as Trustee.
Subordinated Indenture, dated as of March 14, 2014, between JPMorgan Chase & Co. and U.S. Bank Trust National Association, as Trustee.

customers.relationship: Strategic partner for credit card portfolio (Apple Card)
customers.context: On January 7, 2026, JPMorganChase announced that Chase will become the new issuer of Apple Card. The Firm entered into a forward purchase commitment on December 30, 2025 to acquire the Apple credit card portfolio.
Originated mortgage loans are retained in the residential real estate portfolio, securitized or sold to U.S. government agencies and U.S. government-sponsored enterprises.

customers.relationship: Purchaser of mortgage loans for securitization
customers.context: The Firm also sells loans into securitization transactions pursuant to Ginnie Mae guidelines; these loans are typically insured or guaranteed by another U.S. government agency.

geographic_concentration.context: JPMorganChase’s employees are located in 66 countries, with 58% of the Firm’s employees located in the U.S.
At December 31, 2025, $213.1 billion, or 70%, of the total retained residential real estate loan portfolio, was concentrated in California, New York, Florida, Texas and Massachusetts.
The Firm owned or leased facilities in the following locations at December 31, 2025... India 6.6 [million square feet].

supply_chain_risks.context: JPMorganChase also depends on its ability to access and use the operational systems of third parties, including: custodians; vendors, including providers of security, technology and data and cloud computing services.

Conflicts and hostilities between countries... could result in: sustained disruption to or destruction of infrastructure, including energy and power facilities and undersea cables; disruption of global trade, including retaliatory countermeasures.

Third party incidents such as system breakdowns or failures, misconduct by the employees of such parties, or cyber attacks, including ransomware and supply-chain compromises, could have a material adverse effect on the Firm.

geographic_revenue.context: North America revenue was $139,689 million out of a total $182,447 million for the year ended December 31, 2025.
Europe/Middle East/Africa revenue was $24,478 million for the year ended December 31, 2025.
Asia-Pacific revenue was $14,065 million for the year ended December 31, 2025.
Latin America/Caribbean revenue was $4,215 million for the year ended December 31, 2025.

purchase_obligations.context: The Firm entered into a forward purchase commitment on December 30, 2025 to acquire the Apple credit card portfolio, with an expected closing in approximately 24 months... At December 31, 2025, includes estimated total credit exposure related to the Apple Card transaction... of approximately $104 billion.
As of December 31, 2025, the Firm had additional future operating lease commitments of $1.9 billion that were signed but had not yet commenced.

market_risk_disclosures.hedging: investment securities portfolio and interest rate derivatives
market_risk_disclosures.context: The Firm manages structural interest rate risk generally through its investment securities portfolio and interest rate derivatives. A +100 bps parallel shift in rates results in a $2.1 billion sensitivity.

market_risk_disclosures.sensitivity: 10% depreciation of currency = $19 million impact on OCI
market_risk_disclosures.context: The Firm has structural non-U.S. dollar FX exposures arising from capital investments, forecasted expense and revenue... Treasury and CIO may hedge certain of these risks using derivatives.

market_risk_disclosures.sensitivity: Not quantitatively disclosed for total portfolio
market_risk_disclosures.context: Commodities derivatives are used to manage the price risk of certain commodities inventories. Gains or losses on these derivative instruments are expected to substantially offset the depreciation or appreciation of the related inventory.
